beats.text: Here's our three-by-three system — matrix A with entries one, two, one, three, eight, one, zero, four, one — and target vector two, twelve, two.
First pivot: the entry at row one, column one — that's one. A highlight marks it.

Eliminate below it. Row two becomes row two minus three times row one. Three, eight, one becomes zero, two, negative two — wait — zero, two, negative two? Check: three minus three is zero, eight minus six is two, one minus three is negative two. And twelve minus six gives six on the right.
Row three already has zero in column one — nothing to do. Move to the second pivot: the entry two at row two, column two. Highlight it.
Eliminate below. Row three becomes row three minus two times row two. Zero, four, one becomes zero, zero, five. And two minus twelve gives negative ten on the right.
Done. Upper triangular matrix U: one, two, one, zero, two, negative two, zero, zero, five. Modified target c: two, six, negative ten. Ready for back substitution.
